message:
Add prefers-reduced-motion support and fix React purity issues

FrankenGlitch:
- Added useReducedMotion() from framer-motion; when the user
  prefers reduced motion, isGlitching is always false and the
  random interval never starts
- Split the single isGlitching state into isHovered (for hover
  trigger) and isRandomGlitching (for random trigger), with a
  derived isGlitching computed from both plus trigger mode. This
  prevents the random interval from interfering with hover state
  and vice versa
- The random-trigger useEffect now properly cleans up both the
  interval and the pending off-timeout via a ref
  (randomOffTimeoutRef), preventing the setState-after-unmount
  warning that could occur if the component unmounted during
  the 150-350ms glitch window
- The "always" trigger mode is now handled via the derived
  isGlitching computation instead of a useEffect, avoiding a
  render-phase side effect

CustomCursor:
- Added useReducedMotion(); returns null (renders nothing) when
  the user prefers reduced motion, since the entire custom cursor
  is a visual effect
- Replaced Math.random() calls inside useMemo with a deterministic
  PRNG function (prng) seeded by particle index. React strict mode
  double-invokes render, and Math.random() during render is
  impure—different values on each invocation cause hydration
  mismatches and unstable particle layouts. The seeded PRNG
  produces identical values across renders
- Pre-computed drift values (drift1, drift2) in the particle
  memoization instead of calling Math.random() inside JSX
  animation props, which was also a purity violation
- Typed DataDebris props from { x: any; y: any } to
  { x: MotionValue<number>; y: MotionValue<number> }
- Removed unused useRef import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
